summary: Testing customer price hiding and RFQ approval workflow. Backend tests PASSED (14/14). Frontend UI tests found critical bugs: isCustomer/isAdmin state not propagating correctly to components.

ui_bugs.issue: Customer sees 'Calculate Price' button instead of 'Generate RFQ' button. isCustomer condition returning false even though user role is 'customer' in localStorage.
ui_bugs.root_cause: useAuth() hook user state not propagating correctly after login

ui_bugs.issue: Admin filter tabs (All, Pending RFQ, Approved) NOT showing. isAdmin condition returning false even though user role is 'admin' in localStorage.
ui_bugs.root_cause: useAuth() hook user state not propagating correctly after login

ui_bugs.issue: 'Approve & Generate Quote' button not visible for admin on pending RFQs
ui_bugs.root_cause: Same root cause - isAdmin state not correct

integration_issues.issue: Prices visible in Quotes list for customers - Rs. amounts showing where they should be hidden

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix AuthContext user state propagation - useAuth() hook returns null/stale user object
Investigate why isCustomer and isAdmin variables in calculator.tsx and quotes.tsx return false despite correct localStorage data
Consider adding force re-render or using useEffect to listen for user state changes
Add debug logging to verify user object in useAuth() at render time
critical_code_review_comments: Backend RFQ approval endpoint works correctly - tested with curl and pytest
Frontend isCustomer/isAdmin checks exist but fail due to stale user state from useAuth()
AuthContext.tsx loadUser() is async - components may render before user state is loaded
Potential fix: Add loading gate in components or use effect with user dependency

success_rate.frontend: 30% (UI features not working due to auth state bug)

seed_data_creation: Created multiple RFQs and Quotes during backend tests for testing approval workflow
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend approval endpoint POST /api/quotes/{id}/approve works correctly. Frontend has critical bug where useAuth() user state is not propagating to components - isCustomer and isAdmin return false even when user role is correct. localStorage shows correct user data but React state is stale. Customer sees 'Calculate Price' instead of 'Generate RFQ'. Admin does not see filter tabs or approve buttons.

backend_api_tests.test_customer_calculation_returns_prices: PASSED - API returns prices (UI should hide)

rca_of_the_issue: The useAuth() hook returns user object that is initially null during first render. When loadUser() completes and calls setUser(), React should re-render subscribed components. However, the components seem to capture the initial null value. This could be due to: 1) Stale closure in components, 2) Missing dependency array in useEffect, 3) Context not properly triggering re-renders. The localStorage data is correct (verified via browser console), but the React state from useAuth() is not updating the UI.
